message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata

- bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2 (pager deserialization): open → in_progress, assigned to Codex
- bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4 (snapshot publication plane): in_progress → closed, verified
  by MossyTiger as "Live-tree snapshot publication plane verified"
- bd-26yw7 (JSON1 table-valued functions): open → in_progress
- bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3 (cross-connection certification): enhanced logging contract

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -209,7 +209,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3","title":"T6.7.6.3 Cross-connection, multi-process, rollback, restart, and backend-identity certification suite","description":"Publish the certification-grade proof bundle for MemDatabase retirement from runtime/parity hot paths. This subtask owns deterministic cross-connection and multi-process visibility scenarios, rollback and restart recovery scripts, backend-identity checks, and structured evidence manifests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end scripts cover cross-connection and multi-process visibility, rollback, restart recovery, fallback denial, and backend identity on file-backed storage.
2. Structured logs include `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `process_identity`, `backend_identity`, `restart_phase`, `visibility_epoch`, `shadow_path_touched`, and first-failure diagnostics.
3. Replay instructions and artifact hashes are published and suitable for parity/runtime certification use.
4. The proof pack demonstrates that normal runtime/parity behavior no longer depends on MemDatabase shadow-state in either same-process multi-connection or multi-process file-backed operation.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover cross-connection visibility, rollback semantics, restart recovery, fallback-denial behavior, and backend-identity invariants once shadow-state is removed from ordinary flows.
  - Include adversarial cases for stale readers, aborted writers, restart-after-crash visibility, and multi-process reopen/recovery behavior.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_memdb_retirement.sh` for multi-connection and multi-process file-backed scenarios, rollback/restart flows, backend-identity audits, and fail-fast shadow-path denial.
- Logging contract:
  - Emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `process_identity`, `backend_identity`, `shadow_path_touched`, `restart_phase`, `visibility_epoch`, `rollback_kind`, `fallback_denied`, and first-failure diagnostics.
- User-facing intent:
  - This proof pack should make it impossible to confuse runtime/parity mode with shadow-runtime behavior, even when multiple processes are contending over the same file-backed database.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:12:43.550228429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:41:41.934423364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","memdb-cutover","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","title":"T6.7.7 Split PagerInner and remove single-mutex serialization from steady-state pager work","description":"`crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs` still centers the active pager on a single `Arc<Mutex<PagerInner<_>>>`. Even though concurrent mode intentionally skips the eager `writer_active` gate, page access, allocation, cache interaction, commit preparation, and checkpoint coordination still bottleneck through that coarse shared state. On top of that, `Connection` still carries a `commit_write_mutex`, which means the runtime's concurrency design is ahead of the pager beneath it.

This bead is not merely about shaving lock hold times. It is the path from a bridge architecture toward a genuinely next-gen MVCC-native storage fast path. The goal is not to remove all serialization; some physical commit and checkpoint phases are inherently ordered. The goal is to make the unavoidable critical sections explicit and small, while moving steady-state read/page access, metadata publication, and as much write preparation as possible onto partitioned, page-granular, or read-optimized structures. This is the core storage-side cut that allows page-level MVCC to earn its keep as architecture, not marketing.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The steady-state pager read/write path no longer relies on a single global `Mutex<PagerInner>` for routine page access and write staging.
2. Global writer/checkpoint serialization, where still necessary, is narrowed to clearly documented physical phases with explicit measurements of time spent inside those critical sections.
3. The resulting design introduces a genuinely read-optimized metadata/publication path for steady-state operation, whether via partitioned ownership, snapshot publication, RCU/epoch-like techniques, seqlock-style publication, or another evidence-backed mechanism, instead of leaving the system conceptually single-owner under a thinner mutex wrapper.
4. Deterministic unit/property tests and concurrent e2e workloads prove that the refactor preserves correctness, checkpoint safety, and concurrent-mode default behavior.
5. Benchmarks from T6.7.1 show measurable improvement in multi-writer throughput and/or fairness on workloads previously limited by pager serialization.","notes":"Execution split:
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1`: critical-section measurement and state-partitioning design.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4`: MVCC-native snapshot publication plane.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2`: actual de-serialization refactor.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3`: concurrent-writer fairness/durability proof pack.

Planning intent:
- This parent bead should be interpreted as architectural supersession work, not cosmetic lock cleanup.
- Preserve the existing boundary where logical writer coordination lives above the pager; the innovation target is an MVCC-native physical fast path, not a regression to SQLite-style file-lock serialization.
- Parent bead is a milestone umbrella; use child beads for implementation sequencing and evidence gating.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":900,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:02:04.200837231Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:04.253177492Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","title":"T6.7.7.1 Pager critical-section map, timing instrumentation, and state-partitioning design","description":"Instrument and document the current pager critical sections so the refactor is driven by measured serialization points instead of intuition. This subtask owns the critical-section timing fields, state-partitioning design, and invariants for narrowing the global pager lock surface without regressing correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The current pager path has explicit critical-section timing and contention instrumentation tied to stable trace/log fields.
2. The refactor design identifies which state can move off the global mutex, which phases remain serialized, which responsibilities stay in the Connection/MVCC control plane rather than pager/VFS file locks, and which invariants preserve correctness.
3. Deterministic tests validate the instrumentation itself and guard against silent loss of timing/phase metadata.
4. The design is specific enough to guide implementation and future review without re-deriving the pager architecture.","notes":"Planning intent:
- This is a support slice for the promised-land publication work in `bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4`.
- Preserve the existing architectural boundary where logical writer coordination lives above the pager; this bead is about measuring and bounding the current state so the publication-plane refactor can proceed without guesswork.
- Do not treat instrumentation or partition maps as the final win.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealHarbor","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:04.893588399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T09:02:45.305659885Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T09:02:45.305641771Z","close_reason":"Completed pager critical-section instrumentation/design slice; unrelated branch test failures documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":258,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","author":"codex","text":"Audit note: I accidentally closed this bead via shell command substitution while adding a different comment. I immediately restored it to open. No code or issue content changed beyond that mistaken status flip and the restoration.","created_at":"2026-03-07T08:43:07Z"},{"id":259,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","author":"TealHarbor","text":"Implemented pager critical-section instrumentation and state-partitioning map in crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs plus tracing dependency wiring in crates/fsqlite-pager/Cargo.toml. Added stable trace target/field schema, per-phase design metadata, snapshot/reset APIs, and instrumentation across begin/set_journal_mode/set_wal_backend, ensure_writer, get_page, allocate_page, commit, rollback, savepoint capture/rollback, drop cleanup, checkpoint enter/finalize/write/truncate/sync. Added deterministic tests for stable field/design metadata and counter/event recording. Verification: rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-pager --all-targets => pass; rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager --all-targets -- -D warnings => pass; rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => pass. Full rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-pager still has unrelated existing failures on this branch: pager::tests::test_e2e_hot_cold_workload_hit_rate, pager::tests::test_freelist_leak_on_rollback, and s3_fifo::tests::s3_fifo_benchmark_matrix_meets_acceptance_contract (permission error). The new critical-section tests passed within that suite.","created_at":"2026-03-07T09:02:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","title":"T6.7.7.2 Read/write staging refactor off the global PagerInner mutex","description":"Perform the actual pager refactor: move routine page access and write staging off the single global `Mutex<PagerInner>` as far as correctness allows, while keeping any unavoidable physical ordering phases explicit and small. This is the core storage-concurrency implementation slice.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Routine pager read/write staging no longer bottlenecks on a single global `Mutex<PagerInner>`.
2. Any remaining serialized phases are explicit, measured, documented, and small enough to audit rather than incidental.
3. Deterministic unit/integration tests preserve checkpoint safety, concurrent-writer default behavior, pager invariants, and absence of hidden SQLite-style writer serialization.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_pager_deserialization.sh` to exercise deterministic concurrent file-backed workloads, checkpoint overlap, and replayable contention artifacts.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `phase_name`, `hold_ns`, `wait_ns`, `serialized_reason`, `writer_count`, `checkpoint_overlap`, `backend_identity`, and first-failure diagnostics.
6. The implementation does not recreate SQLite-style single-writer blocking under a new name or smear physical ordering phases across ordinary reads/writes.","notes":"User-facing payoff:
- Concurrent write workloads should see less lock-driven latency inflation and better fairness once the pager stops behaving like a hidden choke point.

Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover pager invariants, write-staging correctness, checkpoint safety, and explicit non-regression against hidden writer serialization.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - `scripts/verify_t6_7_pager_deserialization.sh` must emit replay commands and contention artifacts for file-backed conflict-light and conflict-heavy scenarios.
- Logging contract:
  - Remaining serialized phases must be obvious enough that future regressions cannot hide behind vague "pager work" timing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:05.595186845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:41:51.559370484Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

6. The implementation does not recreate SQLite-style single-writer blocking under a new name or smear physical ordering phases across ordinary reads/writes.","notes":"User-facing payoff:

Verification blueprint:

  - Remaining serialized phases must be obvious enough that future regressions cannot hide behind vague "pager work" timing.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:05.595186845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T02:12:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","title":"T6.7.7.3 Concurrent-writer fairness and durability suite with critical-section timing logs","description":"Own the proof bundle for the pager de-serialization work: deterministic concurrent e2e workloads, fairness measurements, checkpoint/durability coverage, and structured logs that expose where time is still spent inside unavoidable critical sections.","acceptance_criteria":"1. End-to-end multi-writer scenarios cover throughput, fairness, checkpoint interaction, and durability on file-backed storage.
2. Structured logs record critical-section timing, queueing/latency symptoms, batch identifiers where relevant, and first-failure diagnostics.
3. Replayable evidence demonstrates that the refactor preserved correctness while reducing time spent in the old choke points.
4. The proof pack is usable as the regression guard for future pager changes.","notes":"Verification blueprint:
- Unit/property layer:
  - Cover pager invariants after lock-surface reduction, checkpoint safety, write-staging correctness, and preservation of concurrent-mode default behavior.
- E2E/replay layer:
  - Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_pager_concurrency.sh` for multi-writer throughput/fairness workloads, checkpoint overlap, restart/durability replay, and lock-contention regression checks.
  - Include deterministic seeds for conflict-heavy and conflict-light workloads.
- Logging contract:
  - Emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `critical_section_name`, `hold_ns`, `wait_ns`, `writer_count`, `checkpoint_overlap`, `backend_identity`, and first-failure diagnostics.
- User-facing intent:
  - Multi-writer users should see both better fairness and clearer evidence about where serialization still remains.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:14:06.306194278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T04:20:15.195341408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","optimization","pager","performance","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","title":"T6.7.7.4 MVCC-native snapshot publication plane for pager metadata and page visibility","description":"Introduce an MVCC-native snapshot publication plane for pager metadata and page-visibility state. Instead of forcing steady-state readers and write staging through shared ownership of coarse pager state, publish reader-consumable snapshots or generation-stamped metadata views using an evidence-backed mechanism such as partitioned ownership, epoch/RCU-style publication, seqlock-style publication, or an equivalent design.

This is the architectural jump from "less locking" to "structurally cheap reads and visibility checks." The exact primitive is not pre-selected, but the outcome is: readers should bind to a coherent published view of relevant pager state without paying hidden global-ownership tax.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Steady-state readers can consult a published pager/visibility snapshot or equivalent metadata view without taking the old global ownership path for ordinary access.
2. Publication invariants are explicit for commit visibility, page-state versioning, checkpoint boundaries, and stale-reader retry behavior.
3. Deterministic unit/property tests cover monotonic publication, stale/superseded reader views, retry behavior, ABA-like hazards, and reclamation safety when applicable.
4. Provide `scripts/verify_t6_7_snapshot_publication.sh` to exercise file-backed concurrent-reader/concurrent-writer scenarios with fixed seeds, deterministic schedules where available, and fallback rejection enabled.
5. Structured logs emit `trace_id`, `run_id`, `scenario_id`, `snapshot_gen`, `visible_commit_seq`, `publication_mode`, `read_retry_count`, `page_set_size`, `elapsed_ns`, and first-failure diagnostics.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- This bead is deliberately frontier-leaning. It is the place to use serious publication techniques if the evidence supports them.
- Do not reduce it to a thinner mutex wrapper; the whole point is a genuinely published metadata plane that makes MVCC visible in the physical runtime structure.
- User-facing payoff: lower read inflation during write pressure, better fairness, and a storage story that is truly differentiated from SQLite's serialized guts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MossyTiger","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-03-06T06:45:57.056859619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T23:31:08.836645258Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T23:31:08.833800822Z","close_reason":"Live-tree snapshot publication plane verified: dedicated gate passes; rch cargo check passes; remaining workspace clippy/fmt failures are unrelated branch drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-uplift","architecture-audit-2026-03","mvcc","next-gen-architecture","optimization","pager","performance","promised-land","storage-wiring","track6","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":260,"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.7.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Review Required (2026-03-08 quality audit)

### Issue: Mechanism Not Finalized
This bead lists design OPTIONS but no DECISION:
- "Partitioned ownership" — epoch-based page ownership per connection
- "Epoch/RCU-style" — read-copy-update for metadata reads
- "Seqlock" — sequence-locked reads for metadata snapshots

### Recommendation
Schedule explicit design review BEFORE implementation begins. The choice affects:
- bd-8.4 (WAL publication plane) which depends on this
- bd-13.3 (hot-page deflection) which is already over-constrained
- bd-9.4 (adaptive fairness) which also depends on this

### Design Decision Criteria
1. Must support concurrent readers without blocking writers (MVCC invariant)
2. Must be compatible with cross-process SharedTxnSlot atomics (no process-local locks)
3. Must not increase commit latency by > 5% (benchmark evidence required)
4. Must integrate with asupersync Cx cancellation (bracket-safe reads)

### Suggested Approach
RCU-style with generation counters is most natural for MVCC metadata:
- Writers publish new metadata under a new generation
- Readers snapshot the current generation at transaction start
- Old generations reclaimed after all readers using them complete
This aligns with existing MVCC snapshot semantics.
","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:20:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","title":"T6.7.8 Make WAL page indexing authoritative and bound refresh/read cost","description":"`fsqlite-wal` still pays too much lookup and refresh tax because the WAL index is not yet authoritative enough for the steady-state path. Reverse scans, capped indexing behavior, and refresh-oriented compensation logic make the runtime pay repeatedly for uncertainty that should instead be resolved once in the write/publication path.

This bead is therefore more than a localized WAL micro-optimization. It is the move that turns WAL metadata into a first-class part of the concurrency architecture: authoritative, incrementally maintained, and cheap for readers and commit coordination to consult without rediscovering state on the hot path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The steady-state WAL read path no longer depends on reverse scans or broad refresh work to resolve page/frame visibility under normal operation.
2. WAL metadata becomes authoritative enough that readers and commit/checkpoint coordination can consult it directly with bounded lookup cost and explicit invariants.
3. Deterministic tests and failure-injection suites cover truncation, torn tail, corruption, recovery, and incremental refresh behavior without semantic drift.
4. Benchmarks from T6.7.1 show reduced WAL lookup/refresh tax and clearer attribution of remaining write-path cost.","notes":"Execution split:
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.1`: generation/index invariants and authoritative lookup contract.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.4`: commit-published WAL visibility plane.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.2`: incremental refresh / steady-state lookup cutover.
- `bd-1dp9.6.7.8.3`: corruption/truncate/torn-tail evidence pack.

Planning intent:
- Treat this as concurrency-substrate work, not just a read-side cleanup task.
- The long-term goal is that WAL metadata publication becomes something the rest of the engine can trust structurally, rather than revalidating through hot-path scans.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":600,"created_at":"2026-03-06T04:02:05.040972610Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:46:04.632424916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture-audit-2026-03","optimization","performance","storage-wiring","track6","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1dp9.6.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:22Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -680,7 +680,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-26qk","title":"Repl: Bench throughput + tail latency (systematic vs decode path)","description":"Benchmark the replication data path so policy knobs (T, overhead%) are data-driven.

## Measurements
- Throughput (MB/s) for:
  - systematic-only path (no loss)
  - mixed path with repair symbols (loss)
- Tail latency (p95/p99) for decode completion under various loss rates.
- CPU cost of:
  - hashing/auth verification
  - symbol generation
  - decode (when invoked)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bench runs are deterministic and recorded (CI-friendly where possible).
- Results feed back into default symbol size + overhead policy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:54:59.943093539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:39:13.927830990Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:39:13.927811343Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic replication throughput/tail-latency benchmarks and compliance tests; full gates green in lane.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26qk","depends_on_id":"bd-15cu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-26qk","depends_on_id":"bd-1lte","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-26rjq","title":"8.4 CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE and FTS3/4/5 Extensions","description":"Establish the `vtab` Rust trait interfaces (`xBestIndex`, `xFilter`) and wire VDBE cursors to dispatch to them. Implement FTS tokenizers (unicode61, porter, trigram) and BM25 ranking.\n\nTESTING & LOGGING:\n- Unit Tests: Verify the `vtab` module trait boundaries with mock implementations.\n- E2E Tests: Build a full-text index, perform `MATCH` queries, and verify `highlight()` and BM25 scoring outputs.\n- Logging: `tracing::info` logging indexing throughput and tokenizer speed during incremental merges.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T07:06:25.182549843Z","created_by":"unknown","updated_at":"2026-02-24T07:06:36.913389312Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26rjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1333e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-26rjq","depends_on_id":"bd-13cyp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-26vi","title":"PARITY-D2: Run quality gates (clippy, fmt, check) after all fixes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:55:10.744848435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:53:57.656159742Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:53:57.656140677Z","close_reason":"Quality gates: cargo check clean, clippy (pedantic+nursery) clean on changed crates, cargo fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26vi","depends_on_id":"bd-12gg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":803,"issue_id":"bd-26vi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# PARITY-D2: Run quality gates (clippy, fmt, check) after all fixes

## Commands

```bash
cargo check --all-targets
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo fmt --check
```

All three must pass with zero errors/warnings.

## Common Clippy Issues to Watch For

The IPK fix adds conditional branching logic. Watch for:
- `clippy::too_many_lines` if the function grows beyond 100 lines
- `clippy::cast_possible_truncation` if casting IPK index to i32
- `clippy::single_match_else` if the if-let pattern should be a match

## Acceptance Criteria

- All three commands exit 0
- No new `#[allow(...)]` annotations unless absolutely necessary (document why)

## Dependencies

Blocked by D1 (bd-12gg) — all tests must pass first.

 {"id":"bd-27cvj","title":"WASM Cross-Cutting: Testing, CI/CD, and Quality Gates for WASM Targets","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:27:49.705410465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T06:45:04.306952825Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27cvj","depends_on_id":"bd-hnzcr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:23Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":804,"issue_id":"bd-27cvj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Cross-Cutting: WASM Testing, CI/CD, and Quality Gates

## Goal
Establish comprehensive testing infrastructure for WASM targets to ensure FrankenSQLite works correctly in browsers and maintains quality as the codebase evolves.

## Testing Strategy
1. **Rust-level wasm tests**: wasm-bindgen-test for unit testing WASM bindings
2. **Browser integration tests**: Playwright/Puppeteer for real browser testing
3. **Conformance tests**: Same SQL conformance suite run against WASM build
4. **Performance benchmarks**: Automated regression detection
5. **Cross-browser matrix**: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge

## CI Pipeline
```yaml
wasm-build → wasm-test → browser-test → package → publish
```
","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:28:00Z"},{"id":805,"issue_id":"bd-27cvj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Cross-Cutting Epic: Testing, CI/CD, and Quality Gates — Detailed Spec

## Goal
This epic defines the quality infrastructure that ensures every WASM bead works correctly in isolation and as part of the full stack. It's the parent for T.1-T.5 test beads and coordinates the overall testing strategy.

## Testing Pyramid for WASM

### Layer 1: Rust Unit Tests (wasm-bindgen-test) — T.1
- Fast, in-headless-browser tests for WASM bindings
- Tests: API surface, type conversions, error handling, SQL execution
- Run time: ~30s per suite, parallelizable
- Coverage target: 100% of public WASM API functions

### Layer 2: Browser Integration Tests (Playwright) — T.2
- Real browser tests for cross-browser compatibility
- Tests: Worker lifecycle, OPFS persistence, cross-tab, error recovery
- Run time: ~5 min per browser, serial within browser
- Coverage target: All user-facing workflows

### Layer 3: SQL Conformance (WASM-adapted) — T.3
- Same SQL test corpus as native, run through WASM
- Tests: Type affinity, expressions, joins, subqueries, JSON extension
- Run time: ~2 min (200+ test cases)
- Coverage target: Parity with native fsqlite-e2e

### Layer 4: CI Pipeline — T.4
- GitHub Actions workflow orchestrating all test layers
- Build → Unit Test → Integration Test → Conformance → Size Check → Publish
- Run time: ~15 min total (parallelized)

### Layer 5: Binary Size Tracking — T.5
- Per-PR size regression detection
- Historical tracking with JSONL
- Budget: 2 MiB gzipped max, 1.5 MiB warning

## Quality Gates (All must pass for merge)
1. `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` — compiles
2. `wasm-pack test --headless --chrome` — Rust WASM tests pass
3. Playwright tests pass in Chromium + Firefox + WebKit
4. SQL conformance: 0 new failures vs baseline
5. Binary size: under budget
6. `cargo clippy --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` — no warnings
7. TypeScript types compile: `tsc --noEmit` on all packages

## Test Data Management
- Each test creates its own database with unique name (timestamp suffix)
- Tests clean up after themselves (DROP TABLE or delete DB)
- No shared state between tests — fully isolated
- OPFS cleanup helper for Playwright tests

## Logging Strategy
All tests use consistent logging format:
```
[{layer}:{suite}:{test_name}] {message}
```
Examples:
```
[T.1:lifecycle:open_close] Opening test database...
[T.2:persistence:reload] Data survived page reload
[T.3:types:integer_affinity] expected: integer, actual: integer
```

## Failure Investigation Workflow
When a test fails in CI:
1. Check CI job summary for quick overview
2. Download Playwright trace (auto-uploaded on failure)
3. Check `results.json` for specific assertion failure
4. Reproduce locally: `npx playwright test --project=chromium --grep "failing test"`
5. If WASM-specific: check wasm-bindgen-test output for panic/abort traces

## Phase Dependencies
```
T.1 (wasm-bindgen-test) ← depends on Phase 2 (bindings exist)
T.2 (Playwright) ← depends on T.1 + Phase 3 (Worker module exists)
T.3 (Conformance) ← depends on T.1 (WASM works at all)
T.4 (CI) ← depends on T.1, T.2, T.3, cross-cutting epic
T.5 (Binary size) ← depends on T.4 (CI pipeline exists)
```

## Acceptance Criteria for Epic Completion
1. All 5 test beads (T.1-T.5) are implemented and passing
2. CI pipeline runs on every PR with all quality gates enforced
3. Binary size tracked with historical data
4. Test coverage spans Rust WASM, browser integration, and SQL conformance
5. Failure artifacts (traces, screenshots, logs) auto-captured in CI
6. Documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md for running tests locally

 {"id":"bd-27jy","title":"CLI REPL statement completion should ignore comments/strings and accept trailing comments","description":"CLI REPL statement completion currently checks only whether the buffer ends with ';'. This fails for e.g. SELECT 1; -- comment, and can mis-handle semicolons inside quoted strings/comments. Fix statement_complete() in crates/fsqlite-cli/src/main.rs to detect semicolons outside strings/identifiers and SQL comments (line and block). Add unit tests covering the tricky cases. Run cargo check/clippy/fmt/test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:46:47.728390459Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:56:19.053469424Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:56:19.053450849Z","close_reason":"Improve CLI REPL statement terminator detection (comments/strings) + add tests; clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
